name: Paal Payasam

steps.instruction: Take a wide, deep-bottomed heavy pan. Add 1 litre milk and 1½ litres water.
Bring to a boil on medium-high flame. Once boiling, add washed raw rice.
Keep cooking on medium-high flame. Because of the large quantity of water, the milk will not bubble over. Stir once every 5 minutes.
The milk mixture will start evaporating and reducing in volume. After 30 to 40 minutes, it would have reduced by approximately half.
After about 1 hour and 15 minutes, the mixture will become creamy in colour and thicker in consistency. Most of the liquid will have evaporated.
Add ½ cup sugar and mix well. Let it boil for 2 minutes.
Switch off the flame and add powdered cardamom. Mix well to combine.
Heat 2 tsp ghee in a small pan. Fry broken cashews on low flame until slightly golden.
Add the fried cashews to the payasam and mix.
Serve warm. The payasam thickens further on cooling.
Alternative Pressure Cooker Method: Roast 2 tbsp raw rice with 1 tsp ghee until fragrant. Coarsely powder the rice (just break it).
Grease a large pressure cooker with a few drops of ghee.
Add 4 cups milk and bring to a boil. Add the powdered rice.
Pressure cook for 1 whistle, then keep on the lowest possible flame for 10 minutes.
Open the cooker, add ½ cup sugar and boil on low flame for another 10 minutes until thick.
Add cardamom powder and fried cashews. Serve.

tips: Use a wide, deep, heavy-bottomed pan to prevent the milk from boiling over and scorching.
The large quantity of water prevents the milk from bubbling over — do not reduce the water.
Stir once every 5 minutes — constant stirring is not needed.
Use full-fat milk for the creamiest result. Low-fat milk may curdle.
The milk slowly caramelizes during the long cooking, giving the payasam its unique light creamy colour and rich flavour.
If using the pressure cooker method, grease the cooker well to prevent burnt bottom and overflow.
Add a small stainless steel plate inside the vessel while boiling to prevent milk from boiling over.
Paal Payasam is best served warm. Refrigerate leftovers and reheat gently before serving.
